articles.title: Secret Hidden Rooms Found Inside the Menkaure Pyramid!
articles.summary: Have you ever wondered if pyramids have secret doors? Scientists just found two hidden air-filled rooms inside the third-largest pyramid at Giza, in Egypt. They used special radar and ultrasound machines (like a doctor uses to see inside your body) to look through the stone without breaking it. The rooms are behind a smooth, polished wall on the eastern side. For years, experts thought there might be a secret entrance there. Now they have real proof! The bigger void is about as tall as a 10-year-old kid and as wide as a door. This could be a hidden entrance that nobody has seen for thousands of years. How cool is that?

articles.title: This Cow Learned to Use a Brush Like a Tool — And She's Super Smart!
articles.summary: Meet Veronika, a Brown Swiss cow who lives on a farm in Austria. She's not just any cow — she's the first cow ever reported to use a tool! When she's itchy, she picks up a long-handled brush with her tongue and scratches herself. But here's the really clever part: she uses the hard bristles for tough spots like her back, and the soft handle for sensitive areas like her belly button. Scientists were amazed. They thought only chimpanzees could use the same tool in different ways. Veronika proves that cows are way smarter than people give them credit for. How cool is that?

articles.title: 132-Million-Year-Old Dinosaur Tracks Found on a Beach in South Africa!
articles.summary: Imagine walking on a beach and finding footprints that are 132 million years old! That's exactly what happened to scientists in South Africa. They were looking for dinosaur teeth, but instead they found more than two dozen dinosaur footprints on a tiny patch of rock near Knysna. These are the youngest dinosaur tracks ever found in southern Africa — 50 million years younger than any others! The tracks are so special because for a long time, scientists thought dinosaurs disappeared from this area after huge volcanic eruptions. Now they know dinosaurs kept living there for millions of years. The footprints are on a rocky beach that gets covered by seawater twice a day. Talk about a race against time!
